channel.title: Cash Grant on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Cash Grant on The Guyana Brief

item.title: Uncle Ramesh Reports — Monday, April 6, 2026
item.description: Uncle Ramesh, calling from Queens, is very impressed with the airstrip and not worried about the river.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh's Take: Six Dialysis Centres, A Special Envoy, And Some People Just Won't Give Credit
item.description: Uncle Ramesh sees six new dialysis centres, a US Special Envoy, and cost recovery on seven oil projects — and wonders why people still complaining.

item.title: Wednesday's Guyana Brief: Noem Lands, Exxon Expands, and the Bridge Is Still Sinking
item.description: A fired US official arrives as a Special Envoy, Exxon wants two more oil projects, and the World Bank says Guyana's reform programme has been quietly gutted. Wednesday's 5-minute news circus.

item.title: Progress Report — March 24, 2026: Roads, Grants, and the Promise Tracker
item.description: The weekly tracker: what got done, what's still pending, and what was promised at some point that we're still waiting on.

item.title: Tuesday's Guyana Brief: Wheelbarrows, Oil Greed, and the Death of a Newspaper
item.description: The Mohameds went to the CCJ, Exxon wants more oil, and somewhere in the background, a newspaper quietly died. Tuesday's 5-minute Guyana news circus.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh's Take: Cash In Hand, Oil On The Rise, And Some People Just Can't Accept A Good Thing
item.description: Uncle Ramesh from Toronto is very pleased with the cash grant, very confident in Exxon, and has some thoughts about people who pay court debts in wheelbarrows.

item.title: YouTube Scripts – March 24, 2026: Wheelbarrows, Oil, and the CCJ
item.description: Two HeyGen-ready YouTube scripts for Tuesday March 24, 2026 — 60-second quick brief and 4-minute full episode.

item.title: Progress Report & Rumour Mill — March 2026
item.description: Tracking the nation's promises, projects, and entirely fictional rumours.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh's Corner — Wednesday, March 19, 2026
item.description: Uncle Ramesh reads only the Chronicle, sees only the bright side, and adds what the Brief missed.

item.title: Wednesday Brief: Oil Hits $100, Mohameds Rejected Again, and Guyana's Last Independent Paper is Gone
item.description: Oil hits triple digits, the Court of Appeal delivers another blow to the Mohameds, Guyana's cash grant starts flowing to people who already have bank accounts, and we say goodbye to Stabroek News.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh Responds: BUDGET 2026 IS HERE! $100,000 For EVERYBODY While Opposition Busy Electing Fugitives!
item.description: Uncle Ramesh celebrates Budget 2026 and the $100,000 cash grant while questioning why the opposition is electing a man facing US extradition charges.
